title: zea mays var. erythrolepis
intro: Zea mays var. erythrolepis, also known as Red Maize or Purple Corn, is a variety of maize that is characterized by its deep reddish-purple color. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Red Maize is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant is rich in anthocyanins and other flavonoids, which have been shown to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Red Maize is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
